steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Use the diameter form
steps.content: The fastest formula here is circumference equals pi times diameter: $$C=\pi d.$$ The problem gives $C=28\pi$, so $$28\pi=\pi d.$$

steps.title: Cancel $\pi$
steps.content: Divide both sides by $\pi$ to get $$d=28.$$ The diameter is 28 centimeters. You do not need to find the radius first unless you choose to use $C=2\pi r$.
